benefits.p: Broccoli sprouts are an exceptionally rich source of glucoraphanin, which is converted into sulforaphane. Sulforaphane activates the Nrf2 pathway, supporting the body's own antioxidant and cellular defence systems against oxidative stress.

ref.s: National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health

benefits.p: Human studies suggest sulforaphane helps regulate inflammatory signalling and supports normal immune function. These effects are thought to contribute to long-term cellular resilience and healthy ageing.
Sulforaphane increases the activity of phase II detoxification enzymes, helping cells process and remove potentially harmful compounds while maintaining normal cellular function.

ref.s: Oregon State University Linus Pauling Institute

benefits.p: Rich in polyunsaturated fats, including linoleic acid and alpha-linolenic acid, which can help support healthy blood lipid profiles when they replace saturated fats as part of a balanced diet.
Provides all nine essential amino acids, making hemp seeds a valuable source of plant protein for maintaining muscle mass, tissue repair and healthy ageing.
A concentrated source of minerals and vitamin E that contribute to normal muscle and nerve function, immune health and protection of cells from oxidative stress.

Cinnamon contains polyphenols with antioxidant activity that help protect cells from oxidative stress. These compounds contribute to the spice's overall health-promoting properties when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

Human studies suggest cinnamon may modestly improve fasting blood glucose and insulin sensitivity, particularly in people with impaired glucose regulation. Effects are generally small and should complement, not replace, healthy dietary habits.

ref.s: Allen RW et al. (2013) Systematic Review & Meta-analysis

benefits.p: Meta-analyses suggest cinnamon may produce modest improvements in triglycerides and total cholesterol in some populations. The evidence is promising but remains less consistent than for established dietary approaches.

Sage provides polyphenols, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which help protect cells from oxidative stress. These antioxidant compounds contribute to the body's normal defence against everyday oxidative damage.
Human studies suggest sage extracts may support aspects of memory and attention, although evidence is still limited and appears to depend on the preparation and dose used.
Sage contains vitamin K, which contributes to normal blood clotting and the maintenance of healthy bones. Although typically consumed in small amounts, it can contribute to overall dietary intake.

ref.s: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements – Vitamin K

benefits.p: A rich source of marine omega-3 fatty acids, which have consistently been associated with cardiovascular health and normal brain function in human studies. Sardines provide substantial amounts of EPA and DHA in a small serving.

ref.s: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health

benefits.p: Sardines provide complete protein containing all essential amino acids. Adequate protein intake supports maintenance of muscle mass, healthy ageing, and overall body function.
Sardines are one of the few foods that naturally provide meaningful amounts of vitamin D. This nutrient supports calcium absorption, bone health, and normal immune function.

When eaten with their soft edible bones, sardines provide substantial amounts of calcium that supports normal bone and muscle function. This makes them an excellent dairy-free source of this essential mineral.

Pomegranate provides ellagitannins, anthocyanins and other polyphenols that help protect cells from oxidative stress. Human studies consistently show these compounds contribute to antioxidant activity and support healthy ageing.

Its polyphenols and potassium support normal cardiovascular function. Clinical research suggests regular pomegranate consumption may help maintain healthy blood pressure and support blood vessel function as part of a balanced diet.
The edible seeds provide dietary fibre, which supports digestive health, helps nourish beneficial gut bacteria and contributes to satiety while also supplying vitamin C for normal immune function.

Traditional sourdough fermentation can reduce the glycaemic impact of bread compared with some conventionally leavened breads. The effect depends on the flour used, fermentation method and final recipe.

Natural fermentation reduces phytate, helping improve the bioavailability of minerals such as iron, zinc and magnesium. Wholegrain sourdough generally provides greater mineral and fibre content than refined varieties.
Sourdough made with wholegrain flour provides complex carbohydrates and dietary fibre that contribute to digestive health and sustained energy intake as part of a balanced diet.

Garlic provides sulphur-containing compounds including allicin derivatives that have been associated in human studies with modest improvements in blood pressure and blood lipid levels as part of a healthy diet.

Garlic is rich in organosulphur compounds that help support the body's antioxidant defences and protect cells from oxidative stress. These compounds are responsible for many of garlic's studied biological effects.
Garlic naturally contains prebiotic fibres such as inulin and fructooligosaccharides that nourish beneficial gut microbes, helping support a diverse and healthy gut microbiome.
